 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.4.2.2","title":"[bootstrap] Complete certificate-selection and provisioning handoff for hardware tokens","description":"Background: once a token session exists, bootstrap still has to turn that session into usable cryptographic material for provisioning. Today that boundary is implicit and easy to hand-wave, which is exactly how hard-fail stubs survive.

Scope: implement certificate and key enumeration, selection, and provisioning handoff for the authenticated token session. Define how the workflow chooses the right certificate or key when several are present, how the chosen material is surfaced to the provisioning layer, and how refusal or mismatch cases are reported when no suitable identity exists. The goal is a real handoff into provisioning, not an opaque token session that later beads must reinterpret.

Acceptance: the workflow can enumerate candidate cert/key material, select or refuse deterministically, and hand validated provisioning input forward with coverage for no-certificate, ambiguous-certificate, unsupported-key-type, provider-error, and successful provisioning cases. Logs must make selection decisions inspectable without exposing private key material.

Execution note: keep the provisioning boundary narrow and typed so downstream beads do not need provider-specific PKCS#11 object trivia.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:51:23.698830Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:05.338754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","pkcs11","provisioning","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.4.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:51:23.698830Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.4.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.4.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:47.678424Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2397,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.4.2.2","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group hardware-token note: keep this track tied to deterministic mocks and the soft-token harness so final proof does not depend on scarce physical devices.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","title":"fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research","description":"# fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a research-focused macro surface that helps agents extract **authentic user opinions** from Reddit in a structured, bounded way.

This is not “LLM analysis inside the connector”; it is data acquisition + packaging so agents can do downstream reasoning safely.

## Scope
### Research macros (compositions)
- `reddit.research.search_threads`:
  - input: query + subreddit scope + time window
  - output: ranked thread summaries (ids, titles, scores, timestamps)
- `reddit.research.collect_evidence`:
  - input: thread ids
  - output: bounded comment excerpts with provenance pointers (post/comment ids)

### Safety/bounds
- Explicit caps:
  - max threads
  - max comments per thread
  - max total bytes returned
- NSFW/quarantine policy must be explicit.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`
- `reddit.research` (macro operations)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- All content is tainted.
- Structured logs include only ids + counts.

## Deliverables
- Canonical output types:
  - `ResearchThreadSummary`
  - `ResearchEvidenceBundle`
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.research.search_threads`
  - `reddit.research.collect_evidence`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - ranking determinism
  - bounds enforcement
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d6d","title":"fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection","description":"# fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable deep triage by fetching **event-level details** for an issue, including stack traces and relevant context, while maintaining strict secret/PII hygiene.

## Scope
### Read operations
- Fetch latest event for an issue (or a specific event id).
- Retrieve:
  - stack trace frames (module/function/line)
  - breadcrumbs
  - tags
  - environment + release
  - request/user context (PII: must be handled carefully)
- Optional: retrieve attachments/artifacts metadata (download is separate + bounded).

### Output shaping
- Return a **sanitized, structured** view that is useful for debugging:
  - include stable identifiers
  - include stack traces (code locations are ok)
  - redact known secret-like fields

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.events.read`
- `sentry.artifacts.read` (if attachments are exposed)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Event payloads are untrusted and may contain secrets/PII.
  - Do not log raw payloads.
  - Apply redaction before returning “rendered” textual summaries.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.event.get`
  - `sentry.event.latest_for_issue`
  - `sentry.event.stacktrace`
- Redaction strategy:
  - allowlist safe fields
  - redact common secret patterns
  - cap maximum returned payload size

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - stack trace parsing and normalization
  - redaction correctness (golden vectors)
  - size limits + early abort
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Returns are useful for debugging without leaking secrets/PII.
- Logs remain metadata-only with correlation IDs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk","title":"[FCP2] Connector Library: V2-Compliant Connectors","description":"# [FCP2] Connector Library: V2-Compliant Connectors

## Goal
Build a library of production connectors that **fully comply** with the FCP V2 platform:
- single-zone binding
- default deny via capabilities
- revocation freshness enforcement
- correct receipts and audit events
- sandbox + network constraints
- no secrets on disk; no secret logging

This epic intentionally assumes the **core platform** exists first.

## Entry criteria (what must exist before connectors are real)
Connectors should not begin until these are available:
- `[FCP2] Connector SDK (Rust)`
- `[FCP2] Connector Manifest (TOML)`
- `[FCP2] Policy Engine` (DecisionReceipts + reason codes)
- `[FCP2] Sandbox + Egress Proxy`
- `[FCP2] MeshNode` (enforcement boundary)
- `[FCP2] Registry + Supply Chain` (install/verify)
- `[FCP2] Conformance` (golden vectors + fuzz + compliance runner)

## Definition of Done
- Each connector has:
  - a manifest with explicit capabilities + sandbox profile
  - a conformance test suite (unit + integration with mocks)
  - E2E compliance run output
  - performance checks where applicable
  - no real external API calls in unit tests

## Notes
- Backwards compatibility is not a goal. Implement the V2 model directly.
- Mechanical compliance checking is part of the conformance suite.

## Success Criteria
- Every connector epic follows `flywheel_connectors-lszk.5` (base beads `.1`..`.4`, state model, NetworkConstraints, etc.).
- Every connector has mock-only unit/integration coverage per `flywheel_connectors-h32`.
- Tier-1 connectors (Telegram, Discord, OpenAI, Anthropic) each have:
  - a complete manifest/capability map
  - provisioning automation (where applicable)
  - passing E2E compliance runs (`flywheel_connectors-e3i9` + `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`).
- No connector requires hard-coded pricing tables; usage metrics are emitted and policy can compute costs externally.
